analysis.summary: The technician attempted to resolve a Microsoft Teams login issue by repeatedly selecting an account from the 'Welcome to Teams' window. Each attempt resulted in the login window briefly disappearing and then immediately reappearing, preventing any progress from being made. This repetitive action continued for the entire duration of the 10-minute recording without any alternative troubleshooting steps.
actions_taken: Repeatedly clicked 'Sai Revanth' account within the Microsoft Teams welcome window to initiate login.
issues_observed: Microsoft Teams login window repeatedly closes and reopens after account selection, preventing successful login.
notable_events: The technician spent the entire 10-minute session repeating the same action without attempting alternative troubleshooting steps.
concerns: Inefficient troubleshooting: The technician continued to repeat the same failed action for an extended period (10 minutes) without changing strategy or attempting diagnostic steps.
Lack of problem-solving initiative: No other tools or methods were used to investigate why the Teams login was failing.

stuck_details: The technician was stuck in a loop from 0:05 to 10:10, repeatedly clicking the 'Sai Revanth' account in the Teams login window. Each click resulted in the window briefly disappearing and then reappearing, preventing login. No alternative actions were attempted.
tech_performance.efficiency_notes: The technician exhibited extremely low efficiency, repeating the same, unsuccessful action for the entire 10-minute duration. There was no methodical approach to troubleshooting; rather, it was a continuous, unvaried attempt to perform the same action despite its clear failure.
coaching_flags: Should initiate alternative troubleshooting steps when an action repeatedly fails (e.g., try clearing cache, reinstalling Teams, checking event logs, trying 'Create or use another account', or investigating network issues).
Avoid spending excessive time (more than 2-3 minutes) repeating a failed action without a change in strategy.
Develop a systematic approach to diagnose and resolve software login issues.
